name: Scene Video Binary
description: Binary yes/no evaluation of scene video prompt quality — does it break a scene into filmable shots with specific motion, camera work, and narrative coverage?

questions.question: Is the output valid JSON with a shots array containing at least 1 shot, each with required fields (shot_number, description, motion_description, camera_movement, duration_seconds)?
Is every shot duration between 4-8 seconds?
Is each shot description a flowing paragraph in present tense — not keywords or bullet points?
Do descriptions show emotions through physical actions ('tears stream', 'jaw clenches') not labels ('she is sad')?
Does every shot have specific, non-trivial camera movement or framing (not just 'static' or empty)?
Do shots follow cinematic progression (establishing → close-up → reaction)?
Do shots collectively cover the full narrative arc of the source scene — not just one moment?
Are characters described with enough visual detail to be recognizable — or properly linked to reference images?
If the scene has dialogue, are lines distributed across the appropriate shots?
Are referenceImages either empty or containing real file paths (not fabricated)?
